Tests error handling for a TypeError thrown by a custom toString function.

# Purpose
This code is a test script that verifies the behavior of a `print` function when it encounters an object with a `toString` method that throws a `TypeError`. The script attempts to call `print` with an object whose `toString` method throws an error, and an empty array. It then uses a `try-catch` block to catch the expected `TypeError`. Inside the `catch` block, the script asserts that the caught error is an instance of `TypeError` and that the error message is "foo". This ensures that the `print` function correctly propagates exceptions thrown by the `toString` method of its arguments.
